commonIssues:
The Maharashtra government suspended issuance of birth and death certificates for registrations made more than one year after the event, following a crackdown on fake certificates.

How to fix:

  • Contact your local Municipal Corporation or Gram Panchayat office directly and ask for the latest SOP under the new rules.
  • Submit a written representation to the Maharashtra Government portal or the District Collector's office.
  • Consult a registered legal practitioner to file an application through the appropriate court if the suspension affects a genuine case.
  • Helpline: 1800-22-6262 (Maharashtra Govt. Toll-Free)

Applicants must now provide robust valid proof (hospital records, cremation receipts, etc.). Applying without valid proof risks criminal charges under the new anti-fraud rules.

How to fix:

  • Gather all original documents: Hospital death summary, MCCD (Medical Certificate of Cause of Death), cremation/burial receipt.
  • Ensure all documents are self-attested and consistent in name/date details.
  • Visit Aaple Sarkar Portal to check the updated checklist before applying.
  • If documents are insufficient, obtain a sworn affidavit from a Notary Public.

Aadhaar is not accepted as primary proof of the date or place of death, requiring applicants to produce additional supporting documents.

How to fix:

  • Submit the Medical Certificate of Cause of Death (MCCD) from the treating hospital as the primary proof.
  • Include cremation/burial ground receipt showing date and place.
  • For home deaths, obtain a certificate from a registered medical practitioner and a Panchanama from the local police station.
  • Reference: CRS Portal – Document Guidelines

Frequent issues with the Centralized Civil Registration System (CRS) portal cause delays in processing applications.

How to fix:

  • Try accessing the portal during off-peak hours (early morning or late evening).
  • Use the CRS Portal on a desktop browser (Chrome/Firefox preferred).
  • Clear your browser cache and cookies before accessing.
  • If the portal is consistently unavailable, submit offline at your local municipal office and obtain an acknowledgement receipt.
  • Report technical issues at: 1800-11-3399 (CRS Helpdesk)

Errors in the name or date of the deceased often lead to rejection, requiring tedious corrections through affidavits and magisterial orders.

How to fix:

  • Cross-check all details against the hospital death record and Aadhaar before submitting.
  • If rejected, obtain a Notarised Affidavit explaining the discrepancy, signed before a Notary Public.
  • Submit the affidavit along with corrected documents to the local Registrar's office.
  • For court orders, contact the District Magistrate's office in your district.
  • Track corrections at: Aaple Sarkar Portal

For delayed registrations, police verification is now mandatory, adding time and complexity to the process.

How to fix:

  • Visit your local police station with all supporting documents and request a verification letter.
  • Submit the police verification report along with your application to the Registrar's office.
  • Track the status of your application at Aaple Sarkar Portal.
  • For grievances regarding police verification delays, contact: Maharashtra Police helpline 100 or 1800-22-6262.
